channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🔐 ‘The threat is there’: Germany to pair P-8s with MQ-9 drones to keep an eye on Russian subs | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The MQ-9B will be the first [unmanned] system we operate with it — the idea is to have those working closely with the aircraft, but step by step,” said Capt. Broder Nielsen, head of the German Naval Aviation Command.

item.title: 📍 Lipsky cited in Reuters on how the G7 agenda is being design to please Trump | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read the full article here. The post Lipsky cited in Reuters on how the G7 agenda is being design to please Trump appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏴 Tran cited in European Policy Center on the size of the euro denominated international transactions | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read full article here. The post Tran cited in European Policy Center on the size of the euro denominated international transactions appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 PHOTOS: The sights of Berlin Air Show 2026 on Day 3 | Breaking Defense
item.description: A selection of photos from the Breaking Defense team on the ground in Berlin.

item.title: 🏴 How Russia Could Escalate | Foreign Policy
item.description: With progress in Ukraine grinding to a halt, Moscow could seek disruption off the battlefield.

item.title: 📍 Paris defense show may be last chance to buy before war, CEO says | Defense News
item.description: Surging defense spending means Eurosatory is bigger than ever this year, with the number of exhibitors rising 30% to more than 2,600, per the organizer.

item.title: 🏛️ Leonardo in ‘advanced’ talks to expand Italian Army’s AW249 combat helicopter fleet | Breaking Defense
item.description: One of the use-cases envisaged for the platform could be to “fly at high-speed and low-level in order to penetrate deep into enemy territory,” Marco Marinoni, head of the AH-249 acquisition program in the Italian Army, said.

item.title: 📍 Heading Off: New Technique Helps Track Grain Smuggling Expansion to Libya | Bellingcat
item.description: On February 15, 2026, the bulk carrier, Grumant (IMO: 9385879) was pictured at the occupied Ukrainian Port of Feodosia on the Crimean peninsula. Satellite imagery suggests it had already been there for several days. It appeared to stock up on grain before departing on a…

item.title: 🌍 Israel’s Elbit Systems, Germany’s Diehl Defence team on SkyStriker pitch for Berlin | Breaking Defense
item.description: Elbit said that the deal will include local manufacturing and assembly of the system with Diehl, to support “the development of sovereign capabilities and strengthening the German defense industry.”

item.title: ⚛️ The Gulf Arab States Need a Shield Built for Limited Trust | War on the Rocks
item.description: Missiles, drones, and maritime disruptions do not stop at national borders. Gulf defense architecture still too often waits for national permission to act. The Gulf Cooperation Council has spent decades building defense institutions, diplomatic forums, and a language of…

item.title: 🌏 From protest to silence: China’s long game in Zambia | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Zambia’s abrupt decision to postpone RightsCon, an annual summit on human rights in the digital age, shocked organisers and those who planned to attend. The summit’s organiser, New York-based advocacy group Access Now, says the ...

item.title: 🌏 Three Characteristics of China’s Criticism of the Takaichi Administration | The Diplomat
item.description: Beijing’s campaign against Tokyo is unlike anything seen before.

item.title: 🌏 Has the Japan-DPRK Pyongyang Declaration Been Abandoned? | The Diplomat
item.description: A quiet revision to Chongryon’s platform carries significant implications for Tokyo.

item.title: 🌏 China’s Edifice Complex | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why Beijing can’t stop wasteful spending.

item.title: ⚛️ The Strange Defeat of Nuclear Deterrence | Foreign Affairs
item.description: And the coming crisis in strategic stability.

item.title: 📍 Bookshelf: the untold story of a UN secretary-general | ASPI Strategist
item.description: U Thant’s rise to become UN secretary-general, the world’s top diplomat, was, to say the least, unlikely. Thant grew up in a small town in what was then rural Burma, at the time part of ...

item.title: 📍 Thai Court Sentences 2 Men to Death For Involvement in 2015 Erawan Shrine Bombing | The Diplomat
item.description: The attack on the popular tourist attraction in central Bangkok killed 20 people and injured around 120 more.

item.title: 🏛️ As F-35 readiness lags, Pentagon seeks $13.7 billion boost: GAO | Breaking Defense
item.description: The F-35’s full mission capable rate fell to to 25 percent in FY25, the government watchdog found.

item.title: 🔐 F-22’s ‘Catfish’ 757 Testbed Spotted Carrying Raptor’s New Infrared Sensor Pod | The War Zone
item.description: The Catfish 757 has spent decades supporting the F-22 program, and that work now extends to testing external stores. The post F-22’s ‘Catfish’ 757 Testbed Spotted Carrying Raptor’s New Infrared Sensor Pod appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Japan and South Korea Navigate a Turbulent World | The Diplomat
item.description: Two neighbors, one alliance, and a great deal of uncertainty.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran war is a game of liar’s poker | Atlantic Council
item.description: The central question has not simply been who possesses power, but who is willing to absorb pain, tolerate risk, and continue escalating when conventional logic suggests restraint. The post The Iran war is a game of liar’s poker appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 What’s the Philippine Senate Drama All About? | The Diplomat
item.description: The country's upper house has become highly polarized, creating doubts about the upcoming impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte.

item.title: 📍 Peru’s Photo-Finish Election | Foreign Policy
item.description: The presidential race could hinge on just a few thousand votes.

item.title: 📍 Don’t wait for a disaster to develop strong US counter-drone capabilities | Atlantic Council
item.description: Security around the World Cup only underscores the need for wider efforts to ensure safety against the increasing risks from drones. The post Don’t wait for a disaster to develop strong US counter-drone capabilities appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ The Army wants to build a better data center. Can they do it? | Defense One
item.description: A call for industry ideas pulled in 200 proposals. The service is moving ahead with data centers, manufacturing upgrades, and more.

item.title: 🌍 War and Circuses | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trump juggles Iran, a World Cup, and a UFC cage fight.

item.title: 🕵️ House vote puts Section 702 on brink of lapse amid fight over acting spy chief | Defense One
item.description: Trump taps Jay Clayton as ODNI, but Democrats want a promise that Bill Pulte won't even be acting spy chief.

item.title: 🌍 Trump Backpedals on Planned Iran Strikes, Citing Deal Progress | Foreign Policy
item.description: But Iranian news reports that Tehran has not yet agreed to a deal.

item.title: 🏴 Senators want a new robot warfare-focused combatant command | Defense One
item.description: A 4-star general would lead the effort, should SASC’s version of the NDAA become law.

item.title: 📍 What Is the Threshold for Action?: A Question Afghanistan Asks the World | The Diplomat
item.description: How long must the Taliban continue internal repression and support for terrorist groups before the international community recognizes the real scale of the threat?
